commentaries.author: Caesarius of Arles
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh In the way of righteousness is life; and in the pathway thereof there is no death. Caesarius of Arles “The paths of those who harbor resentment for an injury lead to death. ” Are these my words, dearest brothers. They are taken from the canonical sacred Scriptures. Therefore in order that we may not be murderers or among the living dead, let us strive to love not only our friends but also our enemies. Then we will be able to meet a kind and merciful Lord with a conscience at ease, in accord with the bond of his pledge.

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: Bye-way. Of vice. Hebrew, "and a way which leadeth to death "or "its paths conduct to death. "(Calmet)

commentaries.author: The Apostolic Constitutions
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh In the way of righteousness is life; and in the pathway thereof there is no death. “The souls of those that bear a settled hatred are to death,” says Solomon. But our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ says in the gospels: “If you bring your gift to the altar, and there remember that your brother has anything against you, leave there your gift before the altar, and go your way; first be reconciled to your brother, and then come and offer your gift to God.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
